records.ety: [F. capital, L. capitalis capital (in senses 1 & 2), fr. caput head. See Chief, and cf. Capital, n.]

defs: Of or pertaining to the head.
quotes: Needs must the Serpent now his capital bruise Expect with mortal pain.

defs: Having reference to, or involving, the forfeiture of the head or life; affecting life; punishable with death; as, capital trials; capital punishment.
quotes: Many crimes that are capital among us.
To put to death a capital offender.
Whatever is capital and essential in Christianity.

defs: Chief, in a political sense, as being the seat of the general government of a state or nation; as, Washington and Paris are capital cities.
Of first rate quality; excellent; as, a capital speech or song.

records.ety: [Cf. L. capitellum and capitulum, a small head, the head, top, or capital of a column, dim. of caput head; F. chapiteau, OF. capitel. See chief, and cf. cattle, chattel, chapiter, chapter.]

defs: The head or uppermost member of a column, pilaster, etc. It consists generally of three parts, abacus, bell (or vase), and necking. See these terms, and Column.
The seat of government; the chief city or town in a country; a metropolis.
Money, property, or stock employed in trade, manufactures, etc.; the sum invested or lent, as distinguished from the income or interest. See Capital stock, under Capital, a.
That portion of the produce of industry, which may be directly employed either to support human beings or to assist in production.
notes: ☞ When wealth is used to assist production it is called capital. The capital of a civilized community includes fixed capital (i.e. buildings, machines, and roads used in the course of production and exchange) and circulating capital (i.e., food, fuel, money, etc., spent in the course of production and exchange).

defs: Anything which can be used to increase one's power or influence.
quotes: He tried to make capital out of his rival's discomfiture.

defs: An imaginary line dividing a bastion, ravelin, or other work, into two equal parts.
A chapter, or section, of a book.
quotes: Holy St. Bernard hath said in the 59th capital.

defs: See Capital letter, under Capital, a.
